title: Kirby Corp's CIO Sells Nearly 4k Shares As Company Elects New Board Director
author: Adé Hennis
description: Kirby Corporation's Vice President and Chief Investment Officer Scott P. Miller sold 3,960 shares worth approximately $478,000 on February 10, 2026, representing 52.62% of his direct holdings. The sale coincided with the company's announcement of Tracy A. Embree as a new Board Director. Despite the insider sale, Kirby reported strong Q4 2025 earnings and has demonstrated five consecutive years of annual growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the insider sale by the CIO could signal reduced confidence, this is offset by strong Q4 2025 earnings that exceeded EPS estimates ($1.68 vs $1.62 expected), five consecutive years of annual growth, and an 18% year-to-date stock increase. The sale represents a significant portion (52.62%) of Miller's holdings, which warrants caution, but the company's operational performance and market position remain solid.

title: Middle East Prefabricated Construction Industry Report 2025: Megaproject Demand, Vision 2030 and Digital Infrastructure Accelerate Off-Site Adoption - Forecast to 2029
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The Middle East's prefabricated construction sector is expanding rapidly driven by megaprojects (NEOM, Red Sea, Qiddiya), Vision 2030 initiatives, and digital infrastructure needs. Steel and light-gauge structures dominate the market, with Turkey, UAE, and Israel leading regional manufacturing. Key growth factors include labor shortages, cost efficiency, climate resilience, and sustainability mandates. The sector is advancing from pilot initiatives to national development strategies, with increasing adoption of BIM, robotics, and modular factory setups across residential, commercial, and industrial applications.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as a global pre-engineered firm partnering with regional specialists to build factory ecosystems in the Middle East prefab market.

title: Kirby Corp's VP Sells 5,429 Insider Shares for $662K
author: Adé Hennis
description: Ronald Dragg, Vice President and Controller at Kirby Corporation, sold 5,429 shares worth approximately $662,338 on February 4, 2026, following the exercise of stock options. The sale represented 34.30% of his direct holdings. Despite the insider sale, Kirby reported strong Q4 FY2025 earnings and has demonstrated five consecutive years of annual growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company reported strong Q4 FY2025 earnings exceeding EPS estimates ($1.68 vs $1.62 expected), achieved five consecutive years of annual growth, and the stock is up 12.5% year-to-date. The insider sale appears routine (tied to option exercise) rather than a bearish signal, and the company maintains a strong market position as the largest U.S. tank barge operator.

title: This 100-Year-Old Stock Market Theory Is Flashing A Buy Signal Again
author: Piero Cingari
description: Dow Theory, a century-old market framework, has triggered a rare buy signal as both the Dow Jones Industrial Average and Dow Jones Transportation Average reached new highs simultaneously. Historically, such signals have preceded 11.1% average 12-month returns in the S&P 500, with stocks finishing higher 83% of the time. Transportation stocks have recently outperformed, with several names posting significant gains over the past three months.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock jumped 49.3% over the past three months, demonstrating strong performance as part of the transportation sector rally triggered by the Dow Theory buy signal.
Surged 60.6% over the past three months, leading transportation sector gains and benefiting from the broader Dow Theory buy signal confirmation.
Climbed 56.4% over the past three months as part of the outperforming transportation sector following the Dow Theory buy signal.
Gained 49.6% over the past three months as a major driver of transportation sector gains following the Dow Theory buy signal.
Jumped 18.3% over the past three months, outperforming broader market indices and reflecting strong transportation sector momentum from the Dow Theory buy signal.
Gained 11% over the past three months, benefiting from transportation sector outperformance triggered by the Dow Theory buy signal.
Rose 7.9% over the past three months and is part of the Dow Theory confirmation signal, indicating sustained uptrend in industrial stocks.
Broad market exposure benefiting from the Dow Theory buy signal, which historically precedes strong forward returns for the S&P 500.

title: Kirby (KEX) Q3 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
author: Motley Fool Transcribing
description: Kirby Corporation reported a strong Q3 2025, highlighting record earnings potential driven by marine transportation and power generation segments. The company expects robust free cash flow, maintains a strong balance sheet, and plans to continue share repurchases.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Record backlog in power generation, improving inland barge utilization from 80% to 87.6%, strong cash flow projections of $620-$720 million, and management's confidence in long-term growth
